LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ADI SIMAYOF'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE VACATED. DEFENDANT HAS 20 DAYS TO RESPOND. ALSO, ANY MONIES RECEIVED BY PREMIER FINANCIAL IS TO BE RETURNED TO THE DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO INCLUDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT DATES AND SEND A COPY TO ATTY. BLANDA. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288, EMAIL:SWONG@SFTC.ORG. (302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 437c, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Alisa J. Kim, Tel. no. 1-510-736-6293 specially appearing for the Plaintiff;
Adi Simayof, Defendant appearing in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Premier Financial Services LLC' motion for summary judgment and in the alternative summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Based on the declaration of defendant Adi Simayof, there are numerous triable disputes which preclude the granting of any portion of this motion. The most notable triable dispute is whether Mr. Siamyof is obligated on the lease which is the subject of all four of Premier's claims. Premier's arguments that this court should disregard Mr. Simayof's declaration run afoul of the most basic rule on summary judgment: the court is not permitted to evaluate credibility or otherwise weigh the evidence, but must only identify whether there is a triable dispute, however improbable it may be. Evidentary Objections 1 through 18 are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
